title: Boot, Partition, Install, Upgrade, Migration, and Recovery · Spiral Incident Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs boot, partition, install, upgrade, migration, and recovery from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reversible operating-system deployment runbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Inventory compatibility, support, data, encryption keys, applications, accounts, drivers, firmware, licenses, network, power, access needs, backup, and rollback before change.
Trace firmware mode, Secure Boot, device order, boot manager, loader, recovery environment, signed media, architecture, and trusted source.
Plan partition tables, system and recovery partitions, volumes, filesystems, labels, mount points, encryption, capacity, and data-preserving boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Deployment Readiness
steps.instruction:
Order the Deployment Readiness CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Deployment Readiness.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Deployment Readiness assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Deployment Readiness, keep Deployment Readiness connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The review variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Inventory compatibility, support, data, encryption keys, applications, accounts, drivers, firmware, licenses, network, power, access needs, backup, and rollback before change.

stimulus.title: Postdeploy Verify case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The review variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Reaching the desktop proves an operating-system deployment is complete.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior. Artifact under review: reversible operating-system deployment runbook.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Postdeploy Verify, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Postdeploy Verify.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.
